title: Parakohleria melastoma
intro: Parakohleria melastoma, also known as the "Melastoma plant", is a medicinal plant species that belongs to the Melastomataceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Parakohleria melastoma include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Parakohleria melastoma is classified as a species of fish in the family Aploactinidae. It is a type of marine fish, specifically a type of spiny devil fish. Within the class Actinopterygii, Parakohleria melastoma belongs to the order Beryciformes. Further categorization places it in the suborder Trachinoidei. This classification is based on its physical characteristics, such as its body shape and the presence of spines.
